Not all Independent symbols were found. Thought there is no official opposition coalition, DAP and PKR had understanding not to clash with each other since 2003 and SNAP and PKR had an electoral pact under the banner (Barisan Bersatu Sarawak). PKR and SNAP pact also incorporated candidates from Malaysian Dayak Congress, an unregistered party. On the urging of other opposition parties especially Keadilan, PAS reduced number of contested seats from 3 to 1. For further reading, please read Welsh, B. (2006). Malaysia’s Sarawak State Elections 2006 Understanding a Break in the BN Armor. National Democratic Institute. https://www.ndi.org/sites/default/files/2118_my_sarawak_090106.pdf .
